Farn Polrucal PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
January 10,2006
Carla Wardlow, Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste.620
Sacramento, CA95814
Re
Your Request for fnformal Assistance
Our FiIe No. I-06-001
Dear Ms. Wardlow:
This letter is in response to your requestl for informal assistance regarding the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") and the provisions of
r"gulutlorr-l 33 29(c)( I ) (copy enclosed). 
2
QUESTION
Maypoliticalparty committees combine the March 22 andMarch 30,2006,pre-
election statements for state and county central committees?
CONCLUSION
Political party committees may combine the March 22 and March 30,2006,pre-
election statements for state and county central committees and may file a single pre-
election campaign statement on March 30 covering the period through March 25,2006.
I You submitted a revised request on January g,20OS.
2 Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, ofthe California Code ofRegulations. Informal assistance does notprovide the requestor
with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83ll4;regulation
1832e(c)(3).)
.:lr"!rj9l


File No. I-06-001
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
Effective September 21,2004, section 842005 of the Political Reform Act was
amended to require state and county political party committees to file pre-election
campaign statements in connection with a state election if the committee receives
contributions totaling $1,000 or more, or makes contributions or independent
expenditures totaling $500 or more during the period covered by the pre-election
statement. (Section 84200.5(0.)
A special state election will be hetd on April 11,2006,to fill avacancy in the 35ft
Senate District, with a special runoff election schedule for June 6, 2006,the date of the
2006 statewide primary election. As a result, state and county political party committees
may be required to file a pre-election campaign statement on March 22 (coveingthe
period through March 17) in connection with the June 6 statewide primary electi,on, and
another pre-election campaign statement on March 30 (covering the period March 18
through March 25) in connection with the April 11 election. The first pre-election
statement in connection with the April 11 election is due on March 2, ioveringthe period
January 1 through February 25,2006.
Section 84205 states:
The Commission mayby regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports required to be
filed by this title.
Pursuant to section 84205, we advise that politicalparty committees may file a
single pre-election campaign statement on March 30 covering the period through March
25,2006.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (e16)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
chr*
Luisa
General Counsel
